nodes.title: Decode safely with a fallback
nodes.why: Malformed percent-encoding should not crash parsing. Returning raw text keeps behavior predictable.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more code, but much safer on bad input.

nodes.title: Return empty object for non-string input
nodes.why: This keeps the helper defensive and easy to call from uncertain input sources.
nodes.alternative: Throw a TypeError for invalid input types.
nodes.tradeoff: More forgiving API, less strict error signaling.

nodes.title: Support inputs with or without leading ?
nodes.why: People pass both full query fragments and plain strings. This handles both cleanly.
nodes.alternative: Require callers to strip ? before calling.
nodes.tradeoff: One normalization step, better developer ergonomics.

nodes.title: Split each pair on the first = only
nodes.why: Values can include =, so splitting once prevents accidental data loss.
nodes.alternative: Use part.split('=') and assume only two parts.
nodes.tradeoff: A little more manual slicing, but correct handling of real-world values.

nodes.title: Convert + to spaces and ignore empty keys
nodes.why: Query strings often use + for spaces. Trimming and skipping empty keys avoids noisy output.
nodes.alternative: Keep keys exactly as-is, including empty names.
nodes.tradeoff: Cleaner result object, but input normalization is opinionated.

nodes.title: Let later duplicates overwrite earlier ones
nodes.why: For repeated keys, this gives deterministic behavior and matches the stated requirement.
nodes.tradeoff: Simple output shape, but earlier duplicate values are discarded.
